title: Robots: Our Smart Helpers
passage:
Robots in Our Daily Lives

Robots are smart machines. Today, we use robots in many different places. They help us at home, in schools, and in big factories. They make our lives easier and faster.

At home, many people have a small cleaning robot. This robot is round and flat. It moves around the floor and cleans up dust. It can clean under beds and tables. People do not need to sweep the floor because the robot does it every day. Some homes also have smart speakers. These speakers use Artificial Intelligence, or AI. You can talk to them. You can say, 'Play some music' or 'What is the weather today?'. The speaker hears your voice and answers your questions quickly.

In some schools, students use small robots to learn. These robots help students learn how to write computer programs. Students can tell the robot to walk, turn left, turn right, or make a sound. It is a fun way to learn science and technology. Teachers love these robots because students enjoy the lessons.

In big factories, robots do heavy and dangerous work. For example, robots help build cars. They can lift heavy metal pieces and put them together. Robots do not get tired, and they do not make mistakes. They can work all day and all night without sleeping. This helps companies make things very fast.

In restaurants, some robots are waiters. They carry food and drinks to the tables. Customers like to see them because they are cute and polite.

In the future, we will have more robots. Some robots will help doctors in hospitals to look after sick people. Other robots will drive cars and buses. Robots are very helpful friends for humans.

questions.explanation: The first paragraph states: 'Today, we use robots in many different places. They help us at home, in schools, and in big factories.'

questions.text: Why do teachers love robots in schools?

options.A: Because they clean the school floor
options.B: Because they make food for students

questions.explanation: The third paragraph states: 'Teachers love these robots because students enjoy the lessons.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: The cleaning robot cleans the floor every day.
questions.explanation: The second paragraph states: 'People do not need to sweep the floor because the robot does it every day.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Smart speakers cost a lot of money.
questions.explanation: The text mentions smart speakers and what they do, but it does not mention how much money they cost.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Robots in factories get tired after five hours.
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'Robots do not get tired...'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Some robots work as waiters in restaurants.
questions.explanation: The fifth paragraph states: 'In restaurants, some robots are waiters.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students use small robots to learn how to write ______________.
questions.explanation: The third paragraph states: 'These robots help students learn how to write computer programs.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: In big factories, robots do heavy and ______________ work.
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'In big factories, robots do heavy and dangerous work.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Robots in factories can work all day and all night without ______________.
questions.explanation: The fourth paragraph states: 'They can work all day and all night without sleeping.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: In the future, robots will help doctors in ______________.
questions.explanation: The last paragraph states: 'Some robots will help doctors in hospitals to look after sick people.'

vocabulary.meaning: dry dirt that is like powder on floors or tables
to clean the floor using a broom
not safe, can cause hurt or damage
a person (or robot) who serves food in a restaurant

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ này, câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy dùng kỹ thuật Scanning để tìm đúng từ khóa (ví dụ: 'round and flat', 'computer programs', 'hospitals') trong văn bản.

2. Điền từ chính xác: Với dạng điền từ, chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Hãy chép lại nguyên văn từ trong bài, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ (thêm/bớt 's' hoặc thay đổi thì của động từ).

3. Phân biệt False và Not Given: Nếu bài viết nói ngược lại thông tin của câu hỏi, chọn FALSE. Nếu bài viết không hề nhắc tới thông tin đó (ví dụ: giá tiền của loa thông minh), chọn NOT GIVEN.
